playbook.intent: Engagement on T&M → client requests fixed-price for next phase → vendor lands a signed amendment without a margin haircut, with frozen requirements baseline, three-point estimate, risk reserves.
playbook.scope: Six-week conversion sprint that runs in parallel to delivery. Daria leads the conversion from time-and-materials to fixed-price for phase 2. Output: requirements baseline frozen, three-point estimate with risk reserves, defensible commercial proposal, signed contract amendment. Margin floor protected.

success-criteria.criterion: Signed contract amendment with no margin haircut
Change-request rate card in place for in-flight changes

stage.name: Phase-2 scope freeze
stage.intent: Lock the requirements before pricing them.

tasks.task: Elicitation interviews on phase-2 outcomes
Prioritise requirements (MoSCoW / RICE)
Trace each requirement to phase-1 context where applicable
Validate scope with client PM + sponsor; capture written sign-off

stage.decision-gate: Advance when client PM signs off requirements doc v2 in writing. Verbal nods do not count.

stage.name: Solution + risk
stage.intent: Assess solution options and their risk envelope.

tasks.task: Solution assessment: build vs buy vs hybrid
Lessons-learned from phase-1 incorporated into phase-2 risk register
Risk register stacked with named owners
Procurement exposure mapped (licenses, sub-contractors)

stage.decision-gate: Advance once risks have named owners + mitigations. Risks without owners stay open and block.

stage.name: Estimate + commercial
stage.intent: Cost the work, defend the margin, draft the commercial pitch.

tasks.task: WBS to leaf level for phase 2
Three-point estimate (P50/P80/P95)
Schedule with milestone payment points
Quality-management plan
Earned-value tracking points
Draft commercial proposal (one-pager)

stage.decision-gate: Advance when P80 + reserves clears the margin floor. If not, scope-cut first, then re-price.

stage.name: Change-control hardening
stage.intent: Wire up the guard rails BEFORE you sign fixed-price.

tasks.task: Communications-management plan tailored to fixed-price ops
Change-control workflow with daily SLA
Change-request rate card (for in-flight requests)
Stakeholder engagement plan for the next 6 months

stage.decision-gate: Advance when the change-control workflow is co-signed by client PM.

stage.name: Negotiation + signature
stage.intent: Close the amendment without giving the margin back.

tasks.task: Internal red-team challenge
Run client negotiation (pricing, payment terms, acceptance criteria)
Capture lessons-learned for the next conversion
File signed amendment + run kickoff communication

stage.decision-gate: Sign only with margin floor cleared AND change-control wired up. Walk if the client wants neither.

extra.value: [Renegotiation of MSA — amendment only, Re-baselining the current T&M phase — focused on phase 2, Replacing the delivery team — same squad continues]
[Current T&M engagement is healthy (no major change-control fights), Phase 2 scope draft exists, Margin floor agreed inside vendor finance]

[Converting without freezing the requirements — guaranteed margin loss, Letting the client treat the amendment as a renegotiation of phase 1, Skipping the rate card — every change request becomes a fight]
['Did we lock requirements BEFORE we priced them?', 'Could we defend the margin to our CFO without flinching?', 'Did we agree the change-control workflow in writing, with rates?']

{human: faion get-content tm-to-fixed-price-contract-conversion --format md, agent: faion get-content tm-to-fixed-price-contract-conversion --format context, json: faion get-content tm-to-fixed-price-contract-conversion --format json}
[{version: 0.1.0, date: 2026-05-20, note: 'Initial draft for F-064 P4 outsource-specialist persona, global angle'}]
